note: Phase 1 seed covering actionlint's canonical 'kind' identifiers (text-based, not numeric). actionlint emits -format=json with each finding carrying a 'kind' field — stdlib-parseable. Shellcheck / pyflakes kinds are pass-throughs and already covered by shared/rules/languages/shell.json + python.json; listed here for provenance with a pass-through marker-style comment but routed to their home linter's bucket via cross-reference, not duplicated into M-buckets here. Security-framed kinds (credentials, script-injection, permissions-scope-over-broad) defer to Hydra R3, which owns CI-pipeline credential-leak + command-injection taxonomy.

correctness_m1.description: Workflow runtime-failure candidates — malformed expressions, undefined job dependencies, invalid cron schedules, missing runner labels that will cause a queued job to stall indefinitely. M1 widens on job-graph reachability; M5 cannot spin up a GitHub runner at zero-dep so these stay at static confidence.

idiom_m7.description: Workflow idiom — prefer current workflow commands, typed inputs, non-deprecated action versions. Routed to M7 (Idiom-fit).

complexity_m7.description: Phase 2 gap — actionlint does not currently surface workflow-graph complexity or matrix-explosion metrics. Seeded empty; revisit when actionlint (or a companion linter) emits these.

naming_m7.description: Phase 2 gap — actionlint does not enforce job/step name conventions. Seeded empty.

testability_m7.description: Phase 2 gap — no testability-of-workflow checks in the current actionlint surface. Seeded empty.

security_defer_to_hydra.description: CI-pipeline credential and script-injection detectors — Hydra R3 owns CWE-798 (hardcoded credentials), CWE-77 (command injection via unescaped ${{ github.event.*.body }}), CWE-250 (over-broad GITHUB_TOKEN scopes). Lich records the actionlint fire and boosts M6 attention weight on the workflow file but does NOT emit a finding.
